project.title: Identification of acetylated proteins in the hearts of mice fed a ketogenic diet
project.projectDescription: Diets influence metabolism and susceptibility to disease. We have previously demonstrated that a ketogenic diet alleviates cardiac pathology, with its cardioprotective effects linked to increased lysine acetylation in the heart. To identify acetylated proteins associated with this diet, we conducted a proteomic analysis using liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry in the hearts of mice fed either a ketogenic diet or a normal chow diet under hypertension. We identified 966 acetylated peptides corresponding to 320 acetylated proteins in the hearts of mice fed a normal chow diet (group A), while 1597 acetylated peptides corresponding to 425 acetylated proteins were identified in the hearts of mice fed a ketogenic diet (group M).
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Mice were subjected to acute high blood pressure induced by transverse aortic constriction. Two days after the induction of acute pressure overload, the mice were randomly divided into two groups: one group was fed a ketogenic diet (group M), while the other was maintained on a standard normal chow diet (group A). Hearts were harvested for analysis four days after the dietary intervention began. Heart tissues from four mice on a ketogenic diet and four mice on a control diet were collected and pooled for the identification of protein acetylation. The samples were homogenized in the lysis buffer containing 8M urea, 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate, and 1x protease inhibitor cocktail, followed by high-speed centrifugation. Protein concentrations were determined using the Branford Assay. Fifteen milligrams of protein from each sample were used for analysis. We performed in-solution trypsin digestion. The resulting peptides were desalted using a C18 column. A small aliquot of the samples was used for protein expression analysis, while the rest of samples was used for the enrichment of acetylation peptides using the PTMScan Acetyl-Lysine Motif [Ac-K] Kit (Cell Signaling Technology). The enriched peptides were analyzed by LC-MS/MS on a Q Exective MS instrument.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS spectra were searched against the Uniprot mouse database using the Sequest search engine, with a false discovery rate for protein and peptide identification of less than 1%.

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: four mice fed a ketogenic diet and four mice fed a control diet
Characteristics[PooledSample]: were collected and pooled for the identification of protein acetylation.
Comment[Separation]: linear gradient from 3% to 30% buffer B over 155 min, followed by a ramp to higher buffer B (up to 50%) over 15 min, then up to 95% over an additional 15 min.
Comment[GradientTime]: total run time of approximately 220 min.
Comment[IonizationType]: Nanospray Flex ion source with a spray voltage of 2 kV and a capillary temperature of 275 °C.
